reforms.description: The Austrian government has made additional funds available for universities, research and development, and thermal insulation, totaling EUR 6 billion over the period 2012-2016 to stimulate growth. This initiative aims to mobilize Austria's innovation potential more effectively.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for R&D is expected to enhance innovation capacity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding has been allocated and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant increase in public funding for R&D, indicating a major policy shift.
reforms.source_quote: Additional funds for universities, full day care schools, research and development and thermal insulation totalling EUR 6 billion over the period 2012-16 have been made available to stimulate growth.

reforms.description: A new programme, Jungunternehmeroffensive, was initiated in 2013 to promote venture and equity capital provision for new firms, supporting the startup ecosystem in Austria.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to enhance access to capital for startups, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been launched and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: While not a major reform, it represents a significant effort to bolster the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Venture and other equity capital provision for new firms as promoted by new programmes (Jungunternehmeroffensive initiated in 2013) should be encouraged.
